npc-critter-stat-abbreviations.description: Abbreviation key for all NPC and critter stat blocks used throughout Section 10.

metahuman-characteristics.description: Average physical characteristics and lifespan for the five main metatypes.

metatype-attribute-modifiers.description: Attribute modifiers applied to base human values when creating non-human metatype NPCs or grunts.

professional-rating.description: Professional Rating descriptions by tier (0–6), used as a dice pool modifier for resisting Social Skill Tests and as the Group Edge pool size.

rows.description: No unit training, unfamiliar with combat as a unit, clumsy and slow reactions. Will turn tail and run when someone goes down.
Some combat experience, seen a little combat, act with purpose and a plan but haven't developed veteran coolness. Retreat if more than 25% of their team is taken out of the fight.
Well trained, fair amount of experience, don't take foolish chances. Withdraw if they take casualties over half their group.
Top professionals who will not break. Fight to the last man or until mission parameters say it is time to withdraw.

prime-runner-advancement.description: Karma advancement multipliers for prime runners by level relative to PC power.

rows.advancementKarma: 80 percent of PC's average Karma award
100 percent of PC's average Karma award
125 percent of PC's average Karma award
200 percent of PC's average Karma award

connection-rating.description: Contact Connection Ratings 1–12 describing social influence and network reach. Characters cannot start the game with a contact whose Connection Rating exceeds 6.

rows.description: Virtually no social influence; useful only for their Knowledge skills.
Has one or two friends with some Knowledge skills, or some minor social influence.
Has a few friends, but not a lot of social influence.
Knows several people in a neighborhood; a borough mayor or a gang leader.
Knows several people and has a moderate degree of social influence; a city councilman or a low-level executive it a small-to-medium corporation.
Known and connected across his state; a city/sprawl mayor or governor, notable fixer, or a mid-level executive in a medium-sized corporation.
Knows a lot of people over a large area, and has considerable social influence; often holds a leadership position in a national corporation.
Well-connected across a multi-state region; an executive in a state government or a national corporation.
Well-connected on his own continent, with considerable social influence; a mid-level executive in a small national government or AA megacorporation.
Well-connected worldwide, with significant social influence; a senior executive in a small national government or a AA megacorporation.
Extremely well-connected worldwide, with significant social influence; mid-level executive position in a major national government or AAA megacorporation.
Global power-player with extensive social influence; holds a key executive position in a major national government or AAA megacorporation.

loyalty-rating.description: Contact Loyalty Ratings 1–6 describing the depth of trust and willingness to assist. Applied as bonus dice when a PC negotiates with the contact.

rows.description: The relationship is purely mercenary, based solely on economics. The people involved may not even like each other, and they won't offer any sort of preferential treatment.
The relationship is still all business, but the parties treat each other with a modicum of mutual respect.

The people in the relationship are friendly, but calling them actual friends might be stretching it. The contact is willing to be inconvenienced in small ways for the character but won't take a fall for him.
There's actual friendship here, or at least solid mutual respect. The contact will go out of his way for the character if needed.
The parties know and trust each other, and have for some time. The contact will back the character even in risky situations.
The contact and character will go to the wall for each other, if that's what it takes.

favor-rating.description: Favor Ratings 1–6 describing the scope and risk of a personal favor a contact might render. Contacts normally agree to favors with a Favor Rating equal to or less than their Loyalty Rating.

rows.description: Deliver a message to someone. Provide access to a low-level restricted security area (airport boarding area, police station).
Loan use of specialized equipment (up to 5,000 nuyen in value). Corporate action requiring the signature of a first-line supervisor.
Provide access to a mid-level restricted security area (standard corporate research lab).
Loan use of specialized equipment (up to 50,000 nuyen in value). Corporate requisition requiring the signature of a middle manager.
Provide access to a high-level security area (FBI offices, AA megacorporate headquarters, AAA regional headquarters).
Loan of specialized equipment (up to 500,000¥ in value). Corporate requisition requiring the signature of a senior manager or junior executive.

search-modifiers.description: Threshold and dice pool modifiers for the Search critter power (Magic + Intuition Extended Test, threshold 5, interval 10 minutes).

rows.situation: Target is more than a kilometer away
Target is a nonliving object or place

toxin-drug-protection.description: Protective gear and their effectiveness against toxin vectors. Protection listed as Immunity (complete protection) or +Rating (bonus dice to Toxin Resistance Test equal to gear's rating).

toxin-costs.description: Availability and nuyen cost per dose for all sample toxins listed in the core rulebook.

toxin-stats.description: Full mechanical statistics for all sample toxins: vector, speed, penetration, power, and effect.

rows.name: CS/Tear Gas
rows.notes: Irritant. Washing with soap and water removes from skin, ending nausea prematurely. Becomes inert after 2 minutes of air contact.

rows.name: Gamma-Scopolamine
rows.notes: Full effects last ~1 hour. After primary effects wear off, residue acts as truth serum for another hour: reduce target's Willpower by 3 (minimum 1) for remainder of effect.

rows.name: Narcoject
rows.notes: Common tranquilizer for dart guns. No side effects.

rows.name: Nausea Gas
rows.notes: Riot control agent. Becomes inert after 2 minutes of air contact.

rows.name: Neuro-Stun VIII
rows.notes: Colorless, odorless. Becomes inert after 10 minutes of air contact.

rows.name: Neuro-Stun IX
rows.notes: Colorless, odorless. Becomes inert after 1 minute of air contact.

rows.name: Neuro-Stun X
rows.notes: Colorless, odorless. Becomes inert after 1 minute of air contact.

rows.name: Pepper Punch
rows.notes: Combines CS and oleocapsicum. Liquid spray. Often tagged with RFID or dyes to identify victims.

rows.name: Seven-7
rows.notes: Colorless, odorless. Developed to bypass chemical protection. Becomes inert after 10 minutes of air exposure.

drug-costs.description: Availability and nuyen cost per dose for all drugs listed in the core rulebook.

drug-stats.description: Full mechanical statistics for all drugs: vector, speed, duration formula, addiction type, and effect.

rows.name: Bliss
rows.notes: Opiate from poppy plants.

rows.name: Cram
rows.notes: Stimulant. Crash is unresisted.

rows.name: Deepweed
effect: Forces Awakened characters to astrally perceive
rows.crashEffect: -1 to all dice pool modifiers and -1 to all limits for same duration as initial effect
rows.notes: Narcotic from Awakened Caribbean kelp. Forces astral perception even on adepts without the ability.

rows.name: Jazz
rows.crashEffect: Disorientation for duration equal to initial effect
rows.notes: Stimulant typically taken from single-dose inhaler.

rows.name: Kamikaze
rows.crashEffect: -1 Reaction, -1 Willpower, -2 to all Limits for equal duration; 6 Stun damage (unresisted)
rows.notes: Tailored combat stimulant. Metabolically destructive with repeated use.

rows.name: Long Haul
rows.crashEffect: Pass out for 8D6 hours. If kept awake during crash: Disorientation with hallucinations and inability to concentrate. Second dose extends wakefulness 1D6+2 additional days then 10 Stun damage (unresisted); cannot stay awake past that point regardless of additional doses.
rows.notes: Cannot keep character awake past second-dose crash with any further doses.

rows.name: Nitro
rows.crashEffect: -2 to all limits, 9 Stun damage (unresisted) for equal duration
rows.notes: Powerful stimulant favored by troll gangers.

rows.name: Novacoke
rows.crashEffect: Charisma and Willpower both reduced to 1, all limits -1 for duration equal to initial effect
rows.notes: Stimulant from coca plants. Highly addictive social drug.

rows.name: Psyche
rows.notes: Designer stimulant for magicians and technomancers. Awakened users suffer only -1 dice pool modifier per sustained spell instead of standard -2.

rows.name: Zen
rows.notes: Psychedelic hallucinogen. Popular among those seeking trance-like states.

btl-costs.description: Availability and nuyen cost per dose for all BTL chip and download types.

addiction-table.description: Addiction Rating and Addiction Threshold for all addictive substances. Addiction Test triggers every (11 - Addiction Rating) weeks of continuous use. Test uses Logic + Willpower (psychological), Body + Willpower (physiological), or both.

rows.addictionRating: total Force of all active foci

sample-grunts.description: Full stat blocks for sample grunts and their lieutenants at each Professional Rating (0–6).

rows.name: Thug Lieutenant
gear: Colt America L36 [Light Pistol, Acc 7, DV 7P, AP -, SA, RC -, 30(c)]

rows.name: Ganger
gear: Browning Ultra-Power [Heavy Pistol, Acc 5 (6), DV 8P, AP -1, SA, RC -, 10(c)]

rows.name: Ganger Lieutenant
gear: Browning Ultra-Power [Heavy Pistol, Acc 5 (6), DV 8P, AP -1, SA, RC -, 10(c)]

rows.name: Corporate Security Guard

Fichetti Security 600 [Light Pistol, Acc 6 (7), DV 7P, SA, RC (1), 30(c)]
Stun baton [Club, Acc 4, Reach 1, DV 9S(e), AP -5, 10 charges]

rows.name: Corporate Security Mage Lieutenant
gear: Fichetti Security 600 [Light Pistol, Acc 6 (7), DV 7P, AP -, SA, RC (1), 30(c)]

rows.name: Police Officer
gear: Ares Predator V [Heavy Pistol, Acc 5 (7), DV 8P, AP -1, SA, RC -, 15(c)]
Defiance EX Shocker [Taser, Acc 4, DV 11S(e), AP -5, SS, RC -, 4(m)]
Stun baton [Club, Acc 4, Reach 1, DV 9S(e), AP -5, 10 charges]

rows.name: Police Lieutenant
augmentations: Cybereyes [Rating 2, w/ flare compensation, image link, low-light vision, smartlink, thermographic vision]
gear: Ares Predator V [Heavy Pistol, Acc 5 (7), DV 8P, AP -1, SA, RC -, 15(c)]
Defiance EX Shocker [Taser, Acc 4, DV 11S(e), AP -5, SS, RC -, 4(m)]
Stun baton [Club, Acc 4, Reach 1, DV 9S(e), AP -5, 10 charges]

rows.name: Organized Crime Soldier
gear: Ceska Black Scorpion [Machine Pistol, Acc 5, DV 6P, AP -, SA/BF, RC (1), 35(c)]
Knife [Blade, Acc 5, Reach -, DV 5P, AP -1] or sword [Blade, Acc 6, Reach 1, DV 7P, AP -2]

rows.name: Organized Crime Technomancer Lieutenant
gear: Beretta 201T [Light Pistol, Acc 6, DV 6P, AP -, SA/BF, RC (1), 21(c)]

rows.name: Elite Corporate Security
augmentations: Cybereyes [Rating 2, w/ flare compensation, image link, low-light vision, smartlink, thermographic vision]
gear: Ares Alpha [Assault Rifle, Acc 5 (7), DV 11P, AP -2, SA/BF/FA, RC 2, 42(c)]
Ares Predator V [Heavy Pistol, Acc 5 (7), DV 8P, AP -1, SA, RC -, 15(c)]
Full body armor [15] & full helmet [+3] (w/ chemical seal)

rows.name: Elite Corporate Security Lieutenant
augmentations: Cybereyes [Rating 3, w/ flare compensation, image link, low-light vision, smartlink, thermographic vision, and vision magnification]
gear: Ares Alpha [Assault Rifle, Acc 5 (7), DV 11P, AP -2, SA/BF/FA, RC 2, 42(c)]
Ares Predator V [Heavy Pistol, Acc 5 (7), DV 8P, AP -1, SA, RC -, 15(c)]
Full body armor [15] & full helmet [+3] (w/ chemical seal)

rows.name: Elite Special Forces
gear: Full body armor [15] & full helmet [+3] (w/ chemical seal)
HK 227 [SMG, Acc 5 (7), DV 7P, AP -4, SA/BF/FA, RC (1), 28(c), w/ APDS ammo]
Smoke grenades (2) [Grenade, DV -, AP -, Blast 10m Radius]
Thermal smoke grenades (2) [Grenade, DV -, AP -, Blast 10m Radius]

rows.name: Elite Special Forces Adept Lieutenant
gear: Full body armor [15] & full helmet [+3] (w/ chemical seal)
HK 227 [SMG, Acc 5 (7), DV 7P, AP -4, SA/BF/FA, RC (1), 28(c), w/ APDS ammo]
Sword weapon focus [Force 2, Blade, Acc 6, Reach 1, DV 11P, AP -2]

sample-contacts.description: Full stat blocks for eight sample contact archetypes.

rows.name: Fixer
placesToMeet: Street corners where surveillance is next to impossible

critter-powers.description: All critter powers with full mechanical details: type, action, range, duration, and effect.

rows.name: Accident
rows.effect: Opposed Test: critter Magic + Willpower vs. target Reaction + Intuition. Critter wins = accident occurs. 4+ net hits = critical glitch severity. Can target up to Magic targets simultaneously.

rows.name: Animal Control
rows.effect: Controls mundane animals. Cannot be used on critters with Sapience. Continues following last orders after leaving LOS. Control: Charisma x 5 small animals OR Charisma large animals.

rows.name: Armor
rows.effect: Natural Armor rating equal to power's rating. Cumulative with worn armor.

rows.name: Astral Form
rows.effect: Critter exists only in astral plane. Can manifest physically like an astrally projecting magician (p.313). Physical attacks/spells have no effect on it.

rows.name: Binding
rows.effect: Sticks target to any surface. Break free: Complex Action, Strength + Body vs. critter Magic + Willpower. Failure = immobilized. Range depends on binding method (LOS/Touch/Self).

rows.name: Compulsion
rows.effect: Opposed Test: critter Magic + Charisma vs. target Willpower + Logic. Target immediately performs compelled action. Cannot plant future-triggered suggestions.

rows.name: Concealment
rows.effect: Subtracts Magic dice from Perception Tests to locate target. Can conceal: Magic metahuman-sized targets, or Magic x 5 small targets.

rows.name: Confusion
rows.effect: Opposed Test: critter Magic + Willpower vs. target Willpower + Logic. Net hits = negative dice pool modifier for all target's actions.

rows.name: Corrosive Spit
rows.effect: Ranged attack: Exotic Ranged Weapon + Agility [Physical]. Range increments = Body meters. DV = (Magic x 2)P. AP = -(critter's Magic).

rows.name: Dragonspeech
rows.effect: Telepathic communication within LOS regardless of Language skills. Cannot be picked up technologically. Targets cannot respond without their own telepathic ability.

rows.name: Dual Natured
rows.effect: Active in both astral and physical planes simultaneously. No -2 dice pool modifier for interacting with physical world while astrally perceiving. Cannot shift between planes.

rows.name: Elemental Attack
rows.effect: Ranged attack: Exotic Ranged Weapon + Agility [Physical]. Range increments = Magic meters. DV = (Magic x 2)P + AP -(critter's Magic) + elemental damage bonus (p.170).

rows.name: Energy Aura
rows.effect: Adds Magic to DV of all critter melee attacks (elemental type specified). Successful melee attacks against critter also deal damage to attacker: DRT, DV = critter's Magic x 2, AP -(critter's Magic).

rows.name: Engulf
rows.effect: Melee attack; DV = Magic x 2. Once engulfed, target trapped and cannot move. Each Action Phase: critter auto-inflicts same damage (resisted with Body + Armor, AP -(critter's Magic)). Escape: Complex Action, Opposed Test target's Strength + Body vs. critter's Magic + Body.

rows.name: Enhanced Senses
rows.effect: Improved sensory capabilities (low-light, thermographic, hearing, smell, sonar, etc.). Senses listed in critter description. If no specific effect for a sense: +2 dice pool modifier for tests using that sense, +1 to appropriate limit.

rows.name: Essence Drain
rows.effect: Permanently drains 1 Essence per Extended Test success from sapient physical being. Requires emotional connection (passion, anger, terror of the attacker). Extended Test: Charisma + Magic (10 - target's Essence, 1 minute). Interruption = no drain. Stolen Essence can boost attributes (Simple Action, +1 per Essence, max +4, lasts 12 hours). Willing victim may become addicted (Psychological Addiction Test, threshold 2).

rows.name: Fear
rows.effect: Opposed Test: critter Willpower + Magic vs. target Willpower + Logic. Terror lasts 1 Combat Turn per net hit. After fear ends, target must pass Willpower + Logic test (threshold = critter net hits) to face the critter again.

rows.name: Guard
rows.effect: Protects against normal environmental accidents/hazards and Accident power effects, can prevent glitches. Can protect up to Magic targets simultaneously.

rows.name: Hardened Armor
rows.effect: If modified DV <= Hardened Armor rating (modified by AP): NO damage, no DRT required. If modified DV > rating: normal DRT applies, with (Hardened Armor / 2, rounded up) as automatic extra hits.

rows.name: Hardened Mystic Armor
rows.effect: Same as Hardened Armor but provides protection against attacks on the astral plane only.

rows.name: Immunity
rows.effect: Hardened Armor equal to twice the critter's Essence against a specific damage type. Subtypes: Immunity to Age (does not age), Immunity to Normal Weapons (all non-magical attacks; does not apply against allergen-based attacks if critter has Allergy weakness).

rows.name: Infection
rows.effect: HMHVV vector. When Essence Drain reduces victim to Essence 0: Opposed Test critter Magic + Charisma vs. victim Body + Willpower. If critter wins: victim infected, enters coma-like state, rises 24 hours later as Infected (critter's original metatype), 1 Essence, insatiable hunger. PC victims become NPCs.

rows.name: Influence
rows.effect: Opposed Test: critter Magic + Charisma vs. target Willpower + Logic. Target acts on suggestion as own idea. Target can resist with Willpower Test (threshold = net hits) to recognize wrongness. Cannot plant future-triggered suggestions.

rows.name: Innate Spell
rows.effect: Instinctively casts a specific spell. Requires Spellcasting skill. Magicians can counter with Counterspelling normally. Produces Drain as normal; -2 penalty for sustaining. Critters/Spirits resist Drain with Intuition or Charisma (GM's discretion).

rows.name: Materialization
rows.effect: Astral critter projects into physical world, forming a temporary body. Gains Immunity to Normal Weapons while materialized. Materializing and dematerializing each require a Complex Action.

rows.name: Mimicry
rows.effect: Exactly imitates sounds including speech. Memory-based imitation requires Memory Test (p.152). Detecting false sound: Perception Test, threshold = critter's Charisma + Magic hits.

rows.name: Mist Form
rows.effect: Transforms into a cloud of mist. Movement: 5 meters per Combat Turn. Cannot use other powers in mist form. Passes through any cracks not airtight. Immunity to Normal Weapons while in mist form. Allergen exposure forces immediate return to normal form.

rows.name: Movement
rows.effect: Modifies target's movement rate by up to critter's Magic (multiply or divide). Only predisposed-to-locomotion targets. One instance per target. Vehicle targets: Magic + Willpower test (threshold = 1/2 vehicle Body, min 2); hits x Acceleration Rating added to/subtracted from Speed.

rows.name: Mystic Armor
rows.effect: Functions as Armor but only provides protection from attacks on the astral plane.

rows.name: Natural Weapon
rows.effect: Claws, teeth, tail, etc. DV and AP listed in critter description. Dual-natured critters can use natural melee weapons against astral targets within reach (uses Unarmed Combat, physical DV). Ranged natural weapons do not work on astral plane.

rows.name: Noxious Breath
rows.effect: Ranged toxin attack: Exotic Ranged Weapon + Agility [Physical]. Vector: Inhalation. Speed: Immediate. Power: critter's Magic. Effect: Stun damage, nausea. Armor useless. Cone of (Body) meters; up to 2 targets within 1 meter of each other.

rows.name: Paralyzing Howl
rows.effect: Affects all within (Magic x 15) meters. Opposed Test: critter Magic + Charisma vs. target Intuition + Willpower. Sound-dampening ratings add to target's test. Each net hit: -1 Reaction and -1 Agility. Lasts (Magic + net hits) Combat Turns. If either drops to 0: target paralyzed (Magic + net hits) minutes. Recovery: 1 Reaction and 1 Agility per minute.

rows.name: Paralyzing Touch
rows.effect: Touch-range version of Paralyzing Howl. Surprised/unaware target = automatic success as melee attack causing no damage. Opposed Test: critter Magic + Willpower vs. target Intuition + Willpower. Otherwise functions identically to Paralyzing Howl.

rows.name: Petrification
rows.effect: Opposed Test: critter Magic + Willpower vs. target Intuition + Willpower. Each net hit: -1 Reaction and -1 Agility. If either drops to 0: target turns to stone (Barrier Rating = critter Magic x 2), unaware of surroundings. Once power ends: normal state resumes, 1 Reaction and 1 Agility per minute.

rows.name: Psychokinesis
rows.effect: Moves objects telekinetically. Strength and Agility = hits scored on Magic + Willpower test. Functions like Magic Fingers spell (p.294).

rows.name: Regeneration
rows.effect: At end of each Combat Turn with any damage: Magic + Body Test; adds Body to hits. Heals that many boxes (Physical overflow first, then Physical, then Stun). Cannot heal: brain/spinal damage, weapon foci damage, combat spell damage, most critter/adept power damage, Drain, allergen damage (while in contact). Incompatible with augmentations.

rows.name: Sapience
rows.effect: Critter is self-aware at or above Homo sapiens level. Skills without Sapience are Untrained (not Unaware). Can learn new skills. Can Awaken with a Magic attribute. Can learn any magical task.

rows.name: Search
rows.effect: Extended Test: Magic + Intuition (5, 10 minutes). Critter must have previously seen the target. See Search Modifiers Table for modifier details. Spirits may search in astral space.

rows.name: Venom
rows.effect: Secretes a toxin. Default stats: Vector: Injection, Speed: 1 Combat Turn, Penetration: 0, Power: Magic, Effect: Physical damage. Individual critters may have different venom attributes.

rows.name: Weather Control
rows.effect: Manipulates local weather conditions (only what is realistically possible in the environment). Peaks when critter completes Magic + Willpower (10, 30 minutes) Extended Test. Critter summons and pushes weather; cannot target specific effects (e.g., cannot aim lightning bolts).

rows.mechanic: Treat as the Allergy negative quality (p.78).
Critter must include specific substance in diet (at least one meal per day). Without it, critter eventually grows sick and dies.
No actual Essence; lose 1 Essence per lunar month naturally. At Essence 0: die within Body + Willpower days unless fed. Non-automatic power use: each use accelerates Essence loss by one week.
Specific condition (lack of air, orichalcum, etc.) forces critter into coma-like suspended animation. Wakes quickly (usually within a minute) once condition is removed.
One or more senses function at half normal range/effectiveness or less; may be reduced to complete loss.
Treat as the Uneducated negative quality (p.87).

Substance/condition: Increase DV of all attacks with the substance by 3. Weapons made of it bypass any Immunities. Damage from it cannot be healed by Regeneration or healing magic; only by natural healing. Condition vulnerability: -3 dice pool modifier to Resistance Test to avoid the condition.

mundane-critters.description: Stat blocks for mundane critters: Dog, Great Cat, Horse, Shark, Wolf.

paracritters.description: Stat blocks for paracritters: Barghest, Basilisk, Cockatrice, Devil Rat, Ghoul, Hell Hound, Sasquatch, Vampire, Eastern Dragon (Sirrush), Feathered Serpent, Western Dragon.

rows.name: Barghest
rows.notes: Natural Habitat: Urban areas in North America and northern Europe, British Isles, Australia, Hong Kong, Nairobi.

rows.name: Basilisk
rows.notes: Natural Habitat: Native to Indonesia; found in temperate and tropical woodlands worldwide near running water.

rows.name: Cockatrice
rows.notes: Natural Habitat: Originally open grassland and scrubland in western Europe; now worldwide.

rows.name: Devil Rat
rows.notes: Natural Habitat: Urban areas worldwide.

rows.name: Ghoul
rows.notes: Statistics given for standard human ghoul. For other metatypes, use Metatype Attribute Modifiers table (p.66) and recalculate Condition Monitors and Limits. Some ghouls may have Adept, Magician, or Mystic Adept qualities.

rows.name: Hell Hound
rows.notes: Natural Habitat: Primarily woodland areas of North America; anywhere domesticated dogs are present.

rows.name: Sasquatch
rows.notes: Reach +1. Some sasquatches may have Adept, Magician, or Mystic Adept qualities. Natural Habitat: Mountainous regions of North America and Asia; also Himalayan ranges (yeti — white fur, otherwise identical).

rows.name: Vampire
rows.notes: ESS '2D6' represents variable Essence (not rolled for tests). Starting Magic = 6 or Essence, whichever is lower. Social Limit 5-9 depending on Essence. Cannot eat anything except metahuman blood. Allergy (Sunlight) damage cannot be healed by Regeneration. -4 dice pool modifier to swimming tests. Some vampires may have Adept, Magician, or Mystic Adept qualities.

rows.name: Eastern Dragon
powers: Enhanced Senses (Hearing, Low-Light Vision, Thermographic Vision, Wide-Band Hearing, Smell)
rows.notes: Always Superhuman Prime Runner. Reach +2. Skills are averages for typical adult. Sirrush is statistically identical to eastern dragon.

rows.name: Feathered Serpent
powers: Enhanced Senses (Hearing, Low-Light Vision, Thermographic Vision, Wide-Band Hearing, Smell)
rows.notes: Always Superhuman Prime Runner. Reach +2. Feathered serpents frequently have a tail stinger or fangs with potent venom.

rows.name: Western Dragon
powers: Enhanced Senses (Hearing, Low-Light Vision, Thermographic Vision, Wide-Band Hearing, Smell)
rows.notes: Always Superhuman Prime Runner. Reach +2. Largest dracoform species. Some specimens have bony plates acting as additional armor.
